extensionDescription.message: Microsoft OneDrive storage provider for the VFS Toolkit.

setupSubtitleSuffix.message: wants to connect to a OneDrive account.

setupHintClientId.message: Leave empty to use the bundled default Azure app. Provide your own only if you've registered a separate app.

setupHintClientIdAdvanced.message: Required Azure app settings: Mobile and desktop applications platform with the https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/nativeclient redirect URI, Allow public client flows = Yes, and delegated Microsoft Graph permissions Files.ReadWrite.All + User.Read + offline_access.

setupErrorPopupClosed.message: The sign-in window was closed before authentication completed.

setupErrorStateMismatch.message: OAuth state mismatch — aborted for safety.

setupErrorNoDrives.message: No drives available for this account.

setupErrorNotReady.message: Please complete sign-in and pick a drive first.

configHintConnName.message: Label shown in the storage picker.

configHintPollInterval.message: How often to check OneDrive for external changes. Set to 0 to disable. Minimum is 60 seconds (the browser's alarm floor).

errorAuth.message: Sign-in failed or expired. Please reconnect in the provider Options page.

errorFileExists.message: A file or folder with that name already exists.

errorFolderExists.message: A folder with that name already exists.

errorHttpDescription.message: Microsoft Graph returned an unexpected HTTP $STATUS$ response.

errorLockedDescription.message: OneDrive reports the item is currently locked by another user or process.

errorRateLimitedDescription.message: OneDrive is throttling requests. Try again in a few moments.

errorQuotaExceededDescription.message: The OneDrive account has no storage space left.

optionsPageSubtitle.message: Manage Microsoft accounts and the add-on connections that use them.

optionsAccountNotice.message: Each account requires an Azure AD app registration (Mobile & desktop applications, public-client enabled) with the redirect URI shown in the Add dialog. One account can back multiple connections across add-ons.

optionsNotice.message: Revoking a connection removes the add-on's access to the associated drive. The underlying account remains signed in.

optionsErrorAccountExists.message: An account for this Microsoft user already exists.
